debug_info.goal:
n : ℕ
k : Fin n
S : List (Call n)
h : allKnow (after n S) k
⊢ n - 1 < S.length

failed_attempts:
induction S generalizing k
  case nil =>
    simp [after, initialState, allKnow] at h
    match n with
    | 0 => exact (Fin.elim0 k)
    | 1 =>
      specialize h 0
      simp at h
    | n+2 =>
      specialize h (k + 1)
      simp [initialState] at h
  case cons c S IH =>
    simp at *
    sorry

induction S generalizing n
  case nil =>
    simp [after, initialState, allKnow] at h
    rcases n with _|n
    · exact k.elim0
    · rcases n with _|n
      · simp
      · have h0 := h ⟨0, by simp⟩
        have h1 := h ⟨1, by simp⟩
        simp at h0 h1
        linarith
  case cons c S IH =>
    simp [after, makeCalls] at h
    sorry

induction S generalizing n k
  case nil =>
    simp [after, allKnow, initialState] at h
    cases n
    · simp
    · case succ n =>
      cases n
      · simp at h
      · specialize h 1
        simp [initialState] at h
  case cons c S' ih =>
    sorry

induction S generalizing k
  case nil =>
    simp [after, initialState, allKnow] at h
    match n with
    | 0 => simp
    | 1 => simp
    | n + 2 =>
      have h0 := h 0
      have h1 := h 1
      simp [initialState] at h0 h1
      -- This case is impossible as k cannot be both 0 and 1
      sorry
  case cons c S' ih =>
    sorry

induction S generalizing k
  case nil =>
    simp [after, allKnow, initialState] at h
    sorry
  case cons c S' ih =>
    simp [after, makeCalls] at h
    sorry

induction S generalizing k
  case nil =>
    simp [after, makeCalls, initialState, allKnow] at h
    sorry
  case cons c S ih =>
    sorry

induction S generalizing n k
  case nil =>
    simp [after, makeCalls, initialState, allKnow, knows] at h
    have : ∀ (j : Fin n), k = j := by intro j; exact h j
    cases n
    · simp
    · case succ n =>
      have : (0 : Fin (n + 1)) = (n : Fin (n + 1)) := by rw [this 0, this n]
      simp [Fin.ext_iff] at this
      omega
  case cons c S' IH =>
    simp [after, makeCalls, makeCall] at h
    sorry
sorry
sorry
induction S generalizing k <;> sorry

induction S generalizing k
  case nil =>
    simp [after, allKnow, initialState] at h
    -- This case is impossible for n > 1. For n = 1, it leads to 0 < 0.
    sorry
  case cons c S' ih =>
    -- Each call can increase the number of agents who know secret k by at most 1.
    sorry
exact allKnow_needs_n_min_one S k h

induction S with
  | nil =>
    simp [after, initialState, allKnow] at h
    omega
  | cons c S' IH =>
    sorry

induction S generalizing k
  case nil =>
    simp [after, allKnow, makeCalls, initialState] at h
    have : ∀ i : Fin n, i = k := by intros i; exact h i
    cases n
    · simp at k
    · case succ n =>
      have h0 : (0 : Fin (n + 1)) = k := this 0
      have hn : (Fin.last n) = k := this (Fin.last n)
      rw [← h0] at hn
      simp [Fin.ext_iff] at hn
      case succ.zero =>
        simp at hn
      case succ.succ =>
        linarith
  case cons c S IH =>
    simp [after, makeCalls] at h
    sorry
sorry

    rcases n with _|n
    · simp at h

      · exact absurd h (by simp [initialState, knows])
  | cons c S ih =>

induction S generalizing k

  case cons c S IH =>

induction S generalizing n
  case nil =>
    simp [after, initialState, allKnow] at h
    obtain rfl : n = 1 := by
      by_contra hn
      have : ∃ i : Fin n, i ≠ k := by
        use ⟨(k.1 + 1) % n, Nat.mod_lt _ (by
          by_contra hzero
          subst hzero
          exact hn (by simp))⟩
        intro h_eq
        injection h_eq with h_eq
        have : n > 1 := by
          by_contra h_le_1
          apply hn
          interval_cases n <;> simp at *
        -- This contradiction proof is becoming too long for a "simple" proof.
        sorry
      rcases this with ⟨i, hi⟩
      specialize h i
      contradiction
    simp at *
    sorry -- Still unprovable for n=1
  case cons c S IH =>
    simp [after, makeCalls] at h
    sorry -- Induction step
sorry

    simp [after, allKnow, initialState, makeCalls] at h

  case cons c S IH =>

  case nil =>
